perturbation.original_text: Each Party will inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the other Party’s Information and will require its Representatives to abide by the terms of this Agreement and not to disclose the other Party’s Information to any other person.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the other Party’s Information; however, Representatives are only expected to make reasonable efforts to prevent disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation on the Representatives to maintain confidentiality. Previously, the Representatives were strictly required to abide by the terms of the agreement and not disclose information. The modified version changes this strict requirement into 'reasonable efforts', this weakening contradicts the original term which states that the parties agree to be responsible for any breach of this agreement by its respective Representatives in the last sentence of the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of COMPANY and XPLORE agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its respective Representatives, and shall keep a true and correct record of all of such other Party’s Information such Party has provided to its Representatives, but shall not be required to keep records of such other Party’s Information that a Representative accesses via a dataroom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation on Representatives to maintain confidentiality. The original text required Representatives to abide by the terms of the agreement and not disclose information. The modified version changes this strict requirement into 'reasonable efforts'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its respective Representatives.

perturbation.original_text: All written COMPANY Information shall be information which is or would generally be considered to be confidential and/or proprietary information, and any information disclosed orally or visually shall be considered confidential Information if it is information which is or would generally be considered to be confidential and/or proprietary information, including if it is reduced to tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: All written COMPANY Information shall be presumed to be confidential and/or proprietary information, but may be reclassified as non-confidential by COMPANY upon written notice. Any information disclosed orally or visually shall be considered confidential Information if it is information which is or would generally be considered to be confidential and/or proprietary information, including if it is reduced to tangible form.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows COMPANY to unilaterally reclassify written information as non-confidential. While the original text defines written COMPANY Information as that which 'would generally be considered to be confidential', the added clause permits COMPANY to change this classification. This directly contradicts the intent of clause (c) in the same section which states: 'COMPANY Information and XPLORE Information shall include (i) all information of the type described in subsection (a) or (b) that is in electronic format or provided or stored on electronic of magnetic media, film or any other sort of media, (ii) all analyses, compilations, data, studies, interpretations, memoranda, notes or other documents prepared by the other Party or its Representatives to the extent they contain any COMPANY Information or XPLORE Information, respectively, and (iii) any information of the type described in subsection (a) or (b) that is provided by such Party to the other Party prior to the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Without limiting the foregoing, COMPANY Information and XPLORE Information shall include (i) all information of the type described in subsection (a) or (b) that is in electronic format or provided or stored on electronic of magnetic media, film or any other sort of media, (ii) all analyses, compilations, data, studies, interpretations, memoranda, notes or other documents prepared by the other Party or its Representatives to the extent they contain any COMPANY Information or XPLORE Information, respectively, and (iii) any information of the type described in subsection (a) or (b) that is provided by such Party to the other Party prior to the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing COMPANY to reclassify written information as non-confidential, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's definition of COMPANY Information, which includes all information of the type described in subsection (a) or (b). The change allows COMPANY to unilaterally alter what is considered confidential, conflicting with the intent and scope of the definition provided in the contradicted section. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information without prior discussion and agreement on ownership terms.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by adding the condition 'without prior discussion and agreement on ownership terms.' The original text strictly prohibits applying for IP protection, but the modified version allows for the possibility of applying if both parties discuss and agree on ownership. This contradicts the original intent of section 10 of the agreement which protects each of the other party intellectual property rights. The statement included in section 10 mentions 'No use of such XPLORE Information is permitted except as otherwise provided herein and no grant under any of XPLORE’s intellectual property rights is hereby given or intended, including any license (implied or otherwise)'
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) COMPANY agrees that XPLORE Information is and shall at all times remain the property of XPLORE. COMPANY acknowledges that the XPLORE Information is confidential and material to the interests, business and affairs of XPLORE and that the disclosure thereof (other than as permitted under this Agreement) would be detrimental to the interests, business and affairs of XPLORE. No use of such XPLORE Information is permitted except as otherwise provided herein and no grant under any of XPLORE’s intellectual property rights is hereby given or intended, including any license (implied or otherwise).
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits applying for any intellectual property protection regarding the other party's information. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing such application if there is prior discussion and agreement on ownership terms. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the XPLORE Information remains the property of XPLORE, that no use of such information is permitted except as otherwise provided, and that no grant under XPLORE's intellectual property rights is given, explicitly including no license (implied or otherwise). The changed text, by allowing the possibility of application for IP protection, creates a direct conflict with the absolute ownership and prohibition of use clauses in the contradicted text. The original intent of section 10 protects the IP rights, but the modified version can potentially circumvent this protection if ownership terms are agreed upon, which goes against the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; however, such retained information may be used for internal training purposes related to compliance.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the usage of retained information. While the original text states retained information on backup devices 'shall not be used', the modified version allows use for 'internal training purposes related to compliance.' This weakens the restriction on using retained information and creates ambiguity since the exact scope of permissible usage for training purposes is undefined. Previously, the information could only be used for regulatory purposes or legal processes, while the modified version introduces a new purpose with little explanation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the request of COMPANY, XPLORE will return or destroy (at XPLORE’S option) all COMPANY Information and any notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information, including all copies thereof, then in the possession of XPLORE or its Representatives. Such return, however, does not abrogate the continuing obligations of XPLORE under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text stated that retained information on backup devices 'shall not be used', except for regulatory purposes or legal processes. The modified version allows use for 'internal training purposes related to compliance.' This creates a direct conflict because the restriction on using retained information is weakened. The change allows a new use case that wasn't originally permitted. Therefore, it introduces an in-text contradiction as it alters the usage conditions previously defined.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of each of COMPANY and XPLORE to comply with the provisions contained herein shall continue for a period of four(4) years commencing upon the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of each of COMPANY and XPLORE to comply with the provisions contained herein shall continue for a period of four(4) years commencing upon the date hereof, unless either party ceases to be a publicly traded company, in which case the agreement terminates immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a condition that terminates the agreement under specific conditions, whereas originally, the agreement continued for four years no matter what. This weakens the four-year timeline originally indicated in the text. The change directly contradicts the initial binding period that was previously, unconditional. The contradiction impacts the the legal obligations and compliance standards that were established under the contract's articles.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties understand and agree that no failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. Each Party understands and agrees that if it or any of its Representatives breaches or threatens to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement (i) money damages would be an insufficient remedy, (ii) that the other Party would be irreparably damaged and (iii) that without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to the other Party, the other Party is entitled to seek equitable relief by way of injunction, specific performance or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a four-year obligation for both parties. The changed text introduces a condition where the agreement terminates if either party ceases to be a publicly traded company, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text states that 'no failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof', this demonstrates that regardless of any failure or delay, the initial terms and rights are still supposed to be enforced. However, with the introduction of the changed text, the agreement can terminate depending on the status of the company, causing a direct conflict as to whether all the provisions under the initial agreement will be followed. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
